hero.label: Made for Corgi Insurance
hero.headline: I want to be the BDR who builds Corgi's pipeline from zero.
hero.subheadline: Corgi is doing something genuinely rare: rebuilding insurance from the ground up with AI as the operating system, not a bolt-on. You've raised $108M and you're moving fast. I want to be in the room where the GTM engine gets built.

whyCompany.title: Rebuilding a $1T industry from scratch is the kind of challenge I want.
whyCompany.body: Most insurance startups are wrappers on top of old carriers. Corgi is the carrier. That's a fundamentally harder and more interesting problem. Getting in at this stage means the reps who join now will define how this company sells for the next decade.

whyCompany.title: You move fast and promote from within — that's exactly what I need.
whyCompany.body: Corgi's track record shows BDRs moving to PE in 3-6 months. I'm not here for a 2-year ramp. I want to earn my way up fast by showing results. The intensity you're describing, 70-100 calls a day, is exactly the environment where I know I'll thrive.

whyCompany.title: I built outbound from scratch at a B2B SaaS startup. I know the grind.
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai I was the first BDR. No playbook, no list, just a CRM and a goal. I helped acquire 15-20 paying clients through cold outreach, LinkedIn sequences, and HubSpot. I've done the volume work and I know what consistency looks like.

quote: I've built pipelines from nothing before. At Corgi, I want to do it at a much bigger scale — for a company that's genuinely changing how insurance works.
